summary: Ukraine and Russia conducted a prisoner exchange returning 160 Ukrainian servicemen home. Russian forces launched ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and Zaporizhzhia, with air defenses reportedly intercepting all missiles targeting the capital. Ukrainian forces struck Russian military support vessels and an S-400 radar complex in occupied Crimea.
key_points: Ukraine and Russia completed a prisoner exchange returning 160 Ukrainian military personnel home. (Source: @UkraineNow/82206)
Russian ballistic missiles targeted Kyiv, with air defenses reporting all missiles shot down. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047)
Ukrainian forces struck Russian military support vessels Volga and Vytka and the ferry Petropavlovsk in occupied Crimea. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)
Russian ballistic missile attacks on Zaporizhzhia injured at least six people. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190)
A Russian ballistic missile strike on a residential building in Izium killed one person and injured three others. (Source: @UkraineNow/82193)
Multiple Shahed drones attacked the Kremenchuk oil refinery, with two drones reported over the facility. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68062)
Threats of Iskander-M/S-300 ballistic missile launches from Russia's Kursk region were reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68075)
NATO Secretary General Rutte acknowledged Ukraine's success in drone strikes against Russian energy infrastructure. (Source: @UkraineNow/82188)

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine and Russia conducted a prisoner exchange returning 160 Ukrainian servicemen home. Russian forces launched ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and Zaporizhzhia, with air defenses reportedly intercepting all missiles targeting the capital. Ukrainian forces struck Russian military support vessels and an S-400 radar complex in occupied Crimea.

points: Ukraine and Russia completed a prisoner exchange returning 160 Ukrainian military personnel home, with a border guard reuniting with his son after a long captivity. (Source: @UkraineNow/82206)

Ukrainian forces struck Russian military support vessels Volga and Vytka and the ferry Petropavlovsk in occupied Crimea; the vessels were designed for deploying underwater surveillance systems and mines. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)
Ukrainian forces also struck weapons and a radar station from an S-400 complex covering the Kerch Strait area. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)
The SBU hit a Russian BM-21 Grad MLRS near the village of Snahost, striking during the moment of its salvo. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598)
Denmark will stop granting asylum to Ukrainian men aged 23 to 60 subject to conscription, stating it will not create legal loopholes weakening Ukraine's defense. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31175)
The European Commission proposed extending temporary protection for Ukrainians until March 2028, excluding men aged 23-60 and those already granted protection. (Source: @UkraineNow/82187)
Zelensky stated Belarus under Russian influence is completing construction of roads and ammunition storage bases along the border with Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/82177)
Russian forces launched ballistic missiles at Kyiv, with air defenses reporting all missiles targeting the capital were shot down. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047)
Russian ballistic missile attacks on Zaporizhzhia injured at least six people. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190)
A Russian ballistic missile struck a residential high-rise in Izium, killing one person, injuring three others, destroying building structures, and starting a fire. (Source: @UkraineNow/82193)
Multiple Shahed drones attacked the Kremenchuk oil refinery and Kremenchuk Thermal Power Plant. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68062, @mon1tor_ua/68066)
Threats of Iskander-M/S-300 ballistic missile launches from Russia's Kursk region were reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68075)
Returned Russian prisoners of war are in Belarus, where ombudsman Lantratova is working with them, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382523)
Kremlin spokesman Peskov stated Russia highly values US willingness to help settle the Ukraine conflict and that no one has refused it yet. (Source: @tass_agency/382507)
Russian ballistic missile struck a residential high-rise in Izium, killing one person and injuring three others. (Source: @UkraineNow/82193)
Threats of ballistic weapon use from the northeast were reported for the eastern regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66450)
Tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66465)
A reactive drone moved from Donetsk region toward southern Kharkiv region, heading toward Lozova. (Source: @kpszsu/66473)
Drones were reported heading toward Kharkiv from the south and northeast, and toward Sumy from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/66430)
A drone moved north of Dnipro on a northwestern course, and another drone passed through Staryi Saltiv in Kharkiv region heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/66429)
A reactive drone from Zaporizhzhia region headed toward Dnipropetrovsk region, on a course for Krynychky and Kamianske. (Source: @kpszsu/66484)
Threats of Iskander-M/S-300 ballistic missile launches from Russia's Kursk region were reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68075)
Drones from Belgorod region, Russia, headed toward northern Kharkiv region, on a course for Bohodukhiv. (Source: @kpszsu/66475)
Drones were reported heading toward Sumy from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/66430)
A reactive drone was reported in southern Sumy region heading toward Poltava region. (Source: @kpszsu/66449)
Two reconnaissance-strike drones were reported over Okhtyrka in Sumy region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98267)
Drones were reported near Okhtyrka, likely reconnaissance, close to Esman and Novhorod-Siverskyi in Sumy region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98257)
Zelensky stated Belarus under Russian influence is completing construction of roads and ammunition storage bases along the border with Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/82177)
Russian ballistic missile attacks on Zaporizhzhia injured at least six people. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190)
Ukrainian forces struck Russian military support vessels Volga and Vytka and the ferry Petropavlovsk in occupied Crimea. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)
Ukrainian forces also struck weapons and a radar station from an S-400 complex covering the Kerch Strait area. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)
A state of emergency of regional scale was introduced in occupied Crimea. (Source: @eRadarrua/98252)

DeepStateUA reported no confirmed evidence of active Russian withdrawal from the Kinburn Spit, with the enemy continuing to use the area for launching Lightning drones toward Mykolaiv and Kherson. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23595)
A reactive drone from Dnipropetrovsk region headed toward Kremenchuk. (Source: @eRadarrua/98271)
An enemy reconnaissance drone was reported in eastern Poltava region near Dykanivka, heading toward Poltava, potentially serving as a designator for enemy strike assets. (Source: @kpszsu/66446)
Russian ballistic missiles targeted Kyiv, with air defenses reporting all missiles shot down. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047)
Threats of Iskander-M/S-300 ballistic missile launches from Russia's Kursk region were reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68075)
Threats of ballistic weapon use from the east and northeast were reported. (Source: @kpszsu/66468, @kpszsu/66450)
Multiple Shahed drones attacked the Kremenchuk oil refinery, with two drones reported over the facility. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68062)
Tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66465)

Multiple drone incursions were reported across northern, eastern, and southern directions, including from Belgorod region toward Kharkiv and from Zaporizhzhia toward Dnipropetrovsk. (Source: @kpszsu/66475, @kpszsu/66484)

NATO Secretary General Rutte acknowledged Ukraine's success in drone strikes against Russian energy infrastructure, noting Russian oil refinery production has decreased by one third. (Source: @UkraineNow/82188)

key_developments: Ukraine and Russia completed a prisoner exchange returning 160 Ukrainian military personnel home, with a border guard reuniting with his son after a long captivity. (Source: @UkraineNow/82206)

Ukrainian forces struck Russian military support vessels Volga and Vytka and the ferry Petropavlovsk in occupied Crimea; the vessels were designed for deploying underwater surveillance systems and mines. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

Russian ballistic missiles targeted Kyiv, with air defenses reporting all missiles shot down; a separate ballistic strike on Zaporizhzhia injured at least six people. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047, @UkraineNow/82190)
A Russian ballistic missile struck a residential high-rise in Izium, killing one person, injuring three others, destroying building structures, and starting a fire. (Source: @UkraineNow/82193)
Multiple Shahed drones attacked the Kremenchuk oil refinery and Kremenchuk Thermal Power Plant, with two drones reported over the facility. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68062, @mon1tor_ua/68066)
Threats of Iskander-M/S-300 ballistic missile launches from Russia's Kursk region were reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68075)

NATO Secretary General Rutte acknowledged Ukraine's success in drone strikes against Russian energy infrastructure, noting Russian oil refinery production has decreased by one third. (Source: @UkraineNow/82188)
sections.threat_assessment: The threat level remains high with continued Russian ballistic missile strikes targeting major Ukrainian cities including Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Izium. Multiple drone incursions across northern, eastern, and southern axes indicate sustained pressure on energy infrastructure and civilian targets. The reported threat of Iskander-M/S-300 launches from Kursk region poses a significant escalation risk. Ukrainian forces demonstrated continued capability to strike high-value targets in occupied Crimea, including naval assets and air defense systems.
sections.regional_response: The European Commission proposed extending temporary protection for Ukrainians until March 2028, though excluding men aged 23-60. Denmark will stop granting asylum to Ukrainian men aged 23-60 subject to conscription. NATO Secretary General Rutte publicly acknowledged Ukraine's operational successes, particularly in drone warfare against Russian energy infrastructure. Zelensky warned that Belarus under Russian influence is completing military infrastructure construction along the Ukrainian border.
sections.intelligence_notes: Budanov indicated a prisoner exchange may occur in the coming days. Zelensky approved a 40-day SBU influence operation on the Russian Federation to force Moscow to end hostilities. DeepStateUA refuted claims of Russian withdrawal from the Kinburn Spit, confirming the area remains an active combat zone. The Russian Foreign Ministry strongly recommended Russian citizens refrain from traveling to Moldova. A state of emergency of regional scale was introduced in occupied Crimea.
